  "concepts": [
    {"id": "c_ai_readiness", "name": "AI readiness", "aliases": ["organizational AI readiness"], "description": "An organization's capability to adopt and derive value from AI, framed by the document as developmental rather than static.", "grounding_span": "drive AI readiness", "location": "Section 5"},
    {"id": "c_org_learning", "name": "Organizational learning", "aliases": [], "description": "The framing that AI success depends on an organization's capacity to learn and adapt, not on technology acquisition.", "grounding_span": "organizational learning", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_tech_first_trap", "name": "Technology-first trap", "aliases": ["technology first trap"], "description": "Deploying AI department by department without linking to enterprise goals, yielding implementations that never reach production.", "grounding_span": "technology-first trap", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c_investment_value_gap", "name": "Investment-value gap", "aliases": ["$252B paradox", "$252 billion paradox"], "description": "The divergence between heavy AI capital allocation and realized enterprise value.", "grounding_span": "investment-value gap", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c_enterprise_ai_capability", "name": "Enterprise AI capability", "aliases": [], "description": "The multi-dimensional organizational capacity to use AI across the enterprise.", "grounding_span": "enterprise AI capability", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_ebit_impact", "name": "EBIT impact", "aliases": ["earnings impact", "value realization"], "description": "Enterprise-level earnings before interest and taxes attributable to AI integration.", "grounding_span": "earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT)", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c_ai_project_failure", "name": "AI project failure", "aliases": [], "description": "The high rate at which AI initiatives fail to reach production or generate value.", "grounding_span": "AI project failure", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_culture_leadership", "name": "Culture & Leadership", "aliases": [], "description": "First pillar of enterprise AI capability.", "grounding_span": "Culture & Leadership", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_human_capital", "name": "Human Capital & Operations", "aliases": [], "description": "Second pillar of enterprise AI capability.", "grounding_span": "Human Capital & Operations", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_data_architecture", "name": "Data Architecture", "aliases": [], "description": "Third pillar of enterprise AI capability.", "grounding_span": "Data Architecture", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_systems_infra", "name": "Systems & Infrastructure", "aliases": ["Systems Infrastructure"], "description": "Fourth pillar of enterprise AI capability.", "grounding_span": "Systems & Infrastructure", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_governance", "name": "Governance & Regulatory Compliance", "aliases": ["AI governance"], "description": "Fifth pillar of enterprise AI capability; matures from ad-hoc to systemic and NIST-aligned.", "grounding_span": "Governance & Regulatory Compliance", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_siloed", "name": "Siloed stage", "aliases": ["Siloed"], "description": "First maturity phase: isolated AI efforts building basic understanding.", "grounding_span": "Siloed", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_integrated", "name": "Integrated stage", "aliases": ["Integrated"], "description": "Second maturity phase: organizations run pilots but many stall (IT Silo Bottleneck).", "grounding_span": "Integrated", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_orchestrated", "name": "Orchestrated stage", "aliases": ["Orchestrated"], "description": "Third maturity phase: AI embedded across the enterprise as coworker requiring governance.", "grounding_span": "Orchestrated", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_agentic_ai", "name": "Agentic AI", "aliases": ["agentic enterprise"], "description": "Autonomous AI systems treated as coworkers requiring onboarding and oversight.", "grounding_span": "agentic AI", "location": "Keywords"},
    {"id": "c_agentic_enterprise", "name": "Orchestrated agentic enterprise", "aliases": [], "description": "Target end-state where AI agents are managed as part of the workforce.", "grounding_span": "orchestrated agentic enterprise", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "c_ai_maturity", "name": "AI maturity", "aliases": [], "description": "The developmental level of an organization's AI capability.", "grounding_span": "AI maturity", "location": "Keywords"},
    {"id": "c_leadership_alignment", "name": "Leadership alignment", "aliases": ["Leadership & Strategic Alignment"], "description": "Cross-functional alignment of leadership on AI strategy; primary determinant of stage transition.", "grounding_span": "leadership alignment", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_org_culture", "name": "Organizational culture", "aliases": ["cultural readiness", "cultural resistance"], "description": "Cultural factors—fear, functional silos—identified as primary impediment to becoming data-driven.", "grounding_span": "Culture shifts from employee fear to functional silos", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_data_fragmentation", "name": "Data fragmentation", "aliases": ["data silos"], "description": "Siloed data disconnected from business operations that compounds AI failure.", "grounding_span": "Data fragmentation compounds the problem", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c_manual_data_prep", "name": "Manual data preparation", "aliases": ["semantic bottleneck"], "description": "Ad hoc manual data prep that consumes up to 90% of project effort.", "grounding_span": "manual preparation consumes up to 90% of project effort", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_shadow_ai", "name": "Shadow AI", "aliases": ["unsanctioned tools"], "description": "Unsanctioned AI usage outside formal governance.", "grounding_span": "shadow AI", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_pilot_poc", "name": "Proof of concept", "aliases": ["pilots"], "description": "Pilot-stage AI initiatives, many of which stall before production.", "grounding_span": "moved beyond proof of concept", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "c_prescriptive_guidance", "name": "Prescriptive maturity guidance", "aliases": ["descriptive vs prescriptive"], "description": "Guidance that explains how to advance between maturity stages, contrasted with descriptive assessment.", "grounding_span": "prescriptive guidance for advancing between stages", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_caio", "name": "Chief AI officer", "aliases": ["CAIO"], "description": "A dedicated C-suite AI role, recommended by the document as a fixed-term rather than permanent mechanism.", "grounding_span": "chief AI officer", "location": "Section 5"},
    {"id": "c_knowledge_graphs", "name": "Knowledge graphs and ontologies", "aliases": ["ontologies", "contextual metadata"], "description": "Semantic data enrichment required by agentic systems at the Orchestrated stage.", "grounding_span": "knowledge graphs, and contextual metadata", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_workflow_redesign", "name": "Workflow redesign", "aliases": [], "description": "Fundamental redesign of workflows associated with the highest EBIT impact.", "grounding_span": "fundamentally redesigned their workflows", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "c_cross_functional", "name": "Cross-functional authority", "aliases": ["cross-functional governance"], "description": "The organizational authority spanning functions needed to drive AI readiness.", "grounding_span": "cross-functional authority", "location": "Section 5"},
    {"id": "c_human_ai_collab", "name": "Structured human-AI collaboration", "aliases": [], "description": "Applied research area informing the developmental readiness framing.", "grounding_span": "structured human-AI collaboration", "location": "Methodology"},
    {"id": "c_scaling_paradox", "name": "The Scaling Paradox", "aliases": [], "description": "Project abandonment spikes as organizations hit structural entanglement while scaling.", "grounding_span": "The Scaling Paradox", "location": "Table 1"},
    {"id": "c_it_silo_bottleneck", "name": "IT Silo Bottleneck", "aliases": [], "description": "The barrier at the Integrated stage where pilots stall in departmental silos.", "grounding_span": "IT Silo Bottleneck", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "c_output_avalanche", "name": "The Output Avalanche", "aliases": [], "description": "Frictionless infrastructure intensifying workloads when output outpaces human review capacity.", "grounding_span": "The Output Avalanche", "location": "Table 1"}
  ],
  "definitions": [
    {"id": "d_high_performers", "term": "high performers", "verbatim_text": "those attributing more than 20% of their EBIT specifically to AI integration", "normative_status": "industry", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "those attributing more than 20% of their EBIT specifically to AI integration", "location": "Section 1"},

    {"id": "d_siloed", "term": "Siloed stage", "verbatim_text": "organizations are building basic understanding of what AI can and cannot do", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "organizations are building basic understanding of what AI can and cannot do", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "d_integrated", "term": "Integrated stage", "verbatim_text": "organizations can run pilots but many stall", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "organizations can run pilots but many stall", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "d_orchestrated", "term": "Orchestrated stage", "verbatim_text": "AI is embedded in how work gets done across the enterprise", "normative_status": "academic", "as_of_date": "2026", "grounding_span": "AI is embedded in how work gets done across the enterprise", "location": "Section 4"}
  ],
  "claims": [
    {"id": "cl_thesis", "claim_text": "AI project failure is fundamentally an organizational learning problem rather than a technology deficit.", "claim_type": "normative", "grounding_span": "AI project failure is fundamentally an organizational learning", "location": "Abstract"},
    {"id": "cl_252b", "claim_text": "Global corporate investment in AI reached $252.3 billion in 2024, a 13-fold expansion since 2014.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Global corporate investment in artificial intelligence reached $252.3 billion in 2024", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl_39ebit", "claim_text": "Only 39% of organizations report any enterprise-level EBIT impact from AI.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "only 39% of organizations report any enterprise-level earnings before interest and taxes (EBIT)", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl_6pct", "claim_text": "Just 6% of firms qualify as high performers seeing significant value from AI.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "just 6% qualify as high performers seeing significant value", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl_92invest", "claim_text": "92% of companies plan to increase their AI investments over the next three years.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "92% of companies plan to increase their AI investments over the next three years", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl_80fail", "claim_text": "Over 80% of AI projects fail, roughly double the rate of non-AI IT initiatives.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "over 80% of AI projects", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl_abandon", "claim_text": "The share of companies abandoning the majority of their AI initiatives before production rose from 17% to 42% year over year.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "abandoning the majority of their AI initiatives before production rose from 17% to 42% year over year", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl_26poc", "claim_text": "Only 26% of companies have moved beyond proof of concept to generate tangible value.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "only 26% of companies have moved beyond proof of concept to generate tangible value", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl_1mature", "claim_text": "Just 1% of C-suite leaders describe their generative AI deployments as mature.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "just 1% of C-suite leaders describe their generative AI deployments as mature", "location": "Section 1"},
    {"id": "cl_84leadership", "claim_text": "84% of industry interviewees cited leadership-driven issues as the primary cause of AI project failure.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "84% of industry interviewees cited leadership-driven", "location": "Section 2"},
    {"id": "cl_91culture", "claim_text": "91% of large-company data leaders identified culture as the primary impediment; only 9% pointed to technology.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "only 9% pointed to technology", "location": "Section 2"},
    {"id": "cl_ciscocult", "claim_text": "Cisco found cultural readiness dropped from 40% to 31% among Chasers even as AI budgets grew.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "from 40% to 31% among Chasers", "location": "Section 2"},
    {"id": "cl_62value", "claim_text": "62% of realized AI value derives from core business functions while IT departments generate just 7%.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "62% of realized AI value derives from core business functions such as operations", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "cl_702010", "claim_text": "Top value-generating organizations allocate 70% to people and processes, 20% to technology, and 10% to algorithms.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "70% to people and processes, 20% to technology, and just 10% to algorithms", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "cl_31policy", "claim_text": "Only 31% of organizations report comprehensive AI policies.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "Only 31% of organizations report comprehensive AI policies", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "cl_ranklast", "claim_text": "AI policy ranked last among 23 readiness factors identified across 52 studies.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "AI policy ranked last among 23 readiness factors", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "cl_88disconnect", "claim_text": "88% of organizations struggle with AI development disconnected from business operations.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "88% struggle with AI development disconnected from business operations", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "cl_perf", "claim_text": "Organizations restructuring leadership generate 50% higher revenue growth and 40% higher return on invested capital relative to peers.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "50% higher revenue growth and 40% higher return on invested capital relative to", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "cl_28x", "claim_text": "Organizations reporting the highest EBIT impact are 2.8 times more likely to have fundamentally redesigned their workflows.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "organizations reporting the highest EBIT impact are 2.8 times more likely", "location": "Section 3"},
    {"id": "cl_13of15", "claim_text": "Of 15 reviewed maturity frameworks, only six offer prescriptive guidance for advancing between stages.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "while only six offer prescriptive guidance for advancing between stages", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "cl_90prep", "claim_text": "At the Siloed stage, manual data preparation consumes up to 90% of project effort.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "manual preparation consumes up to 90% of project effort", "location": "Section 4"},
    {"id": "cl_adoption", "claim_text": "Traditional AI adoption has climbed to 72% and agentic AI has already reached 35%.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "traditional AI adoption has climbed to 72%, agentic AI has already reached 35%", "location": "Section 5"},
    {"id": "cl_gm", "claim_text": "GM's AI-generated seat bracket never entered production because the supply chain could not accommodate the AI-generated geometry.", "claim_type": "empirical", "grounding_span": "could not accommodate the AI-generated geometry", "location": "Section 1"}
  ],
